title: The postwar era, 1945-1950
description: How did the second World War end? How does a planet make sense of atomic warfare?

assessment_items.question_content:
Despite costing 14.5 billion dollars, why do some analysts claim the **GI Bill** to be one of the United States’ best investments?


[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The United States provided tuition incentives and backed home loans, but it did not directly issue loans for the GI Bill.

choices.content: Veterans who took advantage of tuition subsidies ended up making \$10,000-15,000 more per year than those who didn’t, generating lots of tax revenue
The government charged a high interest rate on home and education loans, profiting substantially from the credit it extended to veterans
The GI Bill spurred veterans of World War II to re-enlist in future wars, assuring the military supremacy of the United States

assessment_items.question_content:
Which of the following was one reason why it was difficult for women and African Americans to access the benefits of the **GI Bill** in the postwar period? 

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The GI Bill permitted veterans to enroll at both universities and trade schools.

choices.content: GI Bill benefits were only for university courses, and most women and African Americans wanted to enroll in trade schools
Segregation made it difficult for African Americans to take advantage of education benefits since they were not permitted to enroll at most colleges and universities in the South
Only high-ranking military personnel qualified for GI bill benefits, and African Americans and women rarely were promoted to those roles

assessment_items.question_content:
Besides the fine weather, what drew Americans from the Northeast and the Upper Midwest to the **Sunbelt** states of California and Florida?

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Florida and California do have income taxes.

choices.content: The prevalence of postwar defense plants in those states, and the jobs available there
California and Florida did not require residents to pay income taxes, which made living in those states more affordable
The growth of suburban neighborhoods increased the need for automobiles and the sunbelt states were home to many automobile factories

assessment_items.question_content:
**Levittown**, with its precisely-planted trees, its 27-step assembly-line construction, and its identical floorplans, was the first major example of

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: a private suburban housing development in the postwar era.
a commercial zoning development which attracted manufacturing plants.
government-subsidized housing projects.

assessment_items.question_content:
How were African Americans left out of the **postwar housing boom**?

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The postwar housing boom was in suburbia, not city centers.

choices.content: Restrictive covenants prevented homeowners from selling to minorities and banks denied loans to people of color
African American families preferred to rent rather than own, but this barred them from many of the economic benefits of homeownership
African American families lived primarily in rural areas, and the postwar housing boom mainly expanded housing in city centers

assessment_items.question_content:
The romance of war aside, what contributed to the origins of the **baby boom** during the Second World War?

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The baby boom did not result from government subsidies in childcare.

choices.content: Soldiers would get extra pay if they had families to support
The exigencies of wartime decreased social pressure on couples to marry, leading more children to be born out of wedlock
Government subsidies for childcare led families to have more children

assessment_items.question_content:
What was decided at the **Yalta Conference** in 1945?

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The Non-Aggression Pact between the Soviet Union and Germany was decided in 1939 and lasted until 1941.

choices.content: The Allied powers decided to divide Germany, as well as the city of Berlin, into four administrative zones.
A Non-Aggression Pact was drawn up between Hitler and Stalin to ensure that neither regime would invade the other’s territories.
The United States and Russia would continue to occupy the Middle East and Poland.

assessment_items.question_content:
What’s the difference between a **cold war** and a hot war?

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The "heat" of a war has nothing to do with weather or seasons.

choices.content: A cold war is fought at distance where competing nations never actually attack one another; a hot war is a shooting war directly between two powers.
A cold war is a battle between nations in the northern hemisphere; a hot war is a battle between nations in the southern hemisphere.
A cold war is a war fought in the low temperatures during winter months; a hot war is fought in high temperatures during the summer months.

assessment_items.question_content:
How did Britain, France, and the US counteract the **blockade of West Berlin** in 1948?

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: They organized a massive support mission, flying hundreds of airplanes to deliver food and fuel each day.
They sent ships into the Baltic Sea to counter-attack the northeast zone of Germany that was under USSR control.
Britain, France, and the United States joined NATO to recruit more allies to invade Berlin.

assessment_items.question_content:
George Kennan’s **Long Telegram**, sent from Moscow to the US Secretary of State in 1946, advised that the United States’ policy towards communism should be one of

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Kennan did not advise peaceful co-existence with communism.

choices.content: containment: the United States should do everything in its power to block the advance of Soviet power and the spread of communism.
extinction: the United States should attempt to eradicate communist regimes throughout the world using strategic incursions in communist nations.
peaceful coexistence: Kennan believed that the threat of communism had been exaggerated and the United States should avoid conflict with communist nations.

assessment_items.question_content:
The **Marshall Plan**, named after Secretary of State George C. Marshall, provided

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: It was the Lend-Lease Act, not the Marshall Plan, that loaned weapons to the Allies prior to the United States' entry into World War II.

choices.content: \$13 billion in economic aid to the destabilized countries of war-torn Europe
Loans of weapons for the Allies to fight the Axis powers
Government support for millions of World War II veterans to pursue higher education or vocational training

assessment_items.question_content:
The **North Atlantic Treaty Organization**, or NATO, was organized as

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The international peace-keeping body organized after World War II was the United Nations, not NATO.

choices.content: a defensive alliance between Western European countries and the United States.
an economic alliance between the nations of North America.
an international peacekeeping body dedicated to adjudicating disagreements between nations.
